basic_info.name: Sustainability Awards - Building A Better Future Through Knowledge
basic_info.description: Australia’s longest running and most prestigious award program dedicated to acknowledging and celebrating excellence in sustainable design and architecture.

raw_content.homepage: Sustainability Awards - Building A Better Future Through Knowledge Toggle navigation 2025 Winners News Partners Categories Jury 2026 Gallery Summit Gallery Awards Gallery Contact About Us FAQ �� Summit FAQ – Awards Archive 2025 2025 Winners 2025 Shortlist 2025 Summit 2024 2024 Winners 2024 Shortlist 2024 Summit 2023 2023 Winners 2023 Shortlist 2023 Summit 2022 2022 Winners 2022 Shortlist 2022 Summit 2021 2021 Winners 2021 Finalists 2021 Partners 2021 Summit 2020 2020 Gala 2020 Winners 2020 Finalists 2020 Summit 2020 Partners 2019 2019 Winners 2019 Finalists 2019 Live Program 2019 Gallery 2019 Partners 2018 2018 Winners 2018 Shortlist 2018 Live Program 2018 Gallery 2018 Partners 2017 2017 Winners 2017 Shortlist 2017 Gallery For 20 years, the Sustainability Awards have set the benchmark for excellence in Australia’s built environment — recognising projects, organisations and individuals delivering measurable, lasting sustainability impact. Long before sustainability became standard practice, the Awards championed leadership, innovation and credibility. Now celebrating its 20th anniversary, we remain Australia’s longest-running and most respected program dedicated to sustainable building — trusted by industry and recognised by leaders shaping the future of the built environment. Entries open 29 April 2026. VIEW THE 2025 WINNERS SIGN UP FOR UPDATES Sustainability Awards News The embodied carbon dilemma for commercial buildings: Demolish, retrofit, or rebuild? A panel of architects, designers and sustainability experts explores how retrofit, heritage and adaptive reuse can unlock environmental, social and cultural value, often outperforming demolition on cost, carbon and community impact. Read More Climate change and the insurance industry: What does the future hold? In a climate-changed world, insurance has become one of the clearest signals of risk. In this wide-ranging discussion, architects, researchers and resilience experts unpack how climate risk is rewriting insurance premiums, exposing the limits of building codes, and revealing deep flaws in planning and data systems. Read More Electrifying everything under the Sun In a wide-ranging, and often provocative talk, energy expert Saul Griffith makes the case that electrifying homes, transport and businesses is the most powerful, affordable and achievable way to decarbonise Australia by 2040. Read More Embracing modern construction methods and ideas: From construction site to assembly site In this wide-ranging panel discussion, industry leaders from design, manufacturing and construction explore where prefabrication, modular construction and DfMA are succeeding, where they are falling short, and what must change to unlock their full potential. Read More Tackling Australia's housing crisis: Do we really have a solution? Australia’s housing crisis is often framed as a problem of supply, but a panel of experts argues the reality is far more complex. From mismatched housing types and locations to funding gaps in social housing and cultural resistance to density, the discussion reveals a system struggling to align policy and community needs. Read More Regenerative architecture: Beyond sustainability with biomimicry In this wide-ranging conversation, design industry leaders explore what regenerative design really means, and why it particularly matters now. Moving beyond sustainability and net zero, the panel unpacks lessons from nature, and the need to see buildings as living systems that can heal both the environment and communities over time. Read More Is a circular built environment possible and if so, how? At this year’s Summit, a panel moderated by sustainability consultant Emily Gentillini explored what circularity truly means for the built environment, moving beyond recycling to long-term value, adaptability and regeneration, and underlining the need for systems thinking, certification and cross-sector collaboration to embed circular design at scale. Read More How can AI help with sustainability in the built environment? During the discussion on how AI is influencing the environmental performance of buildings across design, construction and operation, the panellists agreed that AI is already delivering substantial reductions in energy use and embodied carbon; however, they stressed that its effectiveness depends on data quality, leadership and ethical guardrails. Read More View All News Partners We’re partnering with like-minded brands who share our vision for change. Together we’re supporting the ongoing progress of the green building movement in our great nation. Platinum Carbon Neutral Partner Gold Partner Category Partners Strategic Partners Media Partners Partner with us Our packages are meticulously crafted to position our Partners as the premier solution providers within their sponsored categories. This association aligns their brand with award-winning architectural projects and prominent professionals within the Architecture and Design network. Additionally, Partners receive exclusive access to valuable networking opportunities at the Sustainability Summit and Awards gala evening. For more information about partner opportunities that may remain available in 2025, please contact Adrian at adrian.wilson@architectureanddesign.com.au or complete the contact enquiry form below.
